description: Evaluates the technical feasibility and fact-checking skill across different validation scenarios, evidence levels, and verdict outcomes. Each eval targets a specific feasibility dimension or decision point.

evals.name: full-6d-analysis-mixed-evidence
evals.prompt: Validate feasibility for migrating a Java monolith to microservices with Kafka event-driven architecture. The team has 5 years of Java experience but no Kafka experience. Timeline: 12 months.
evals.expected_output: Full 6-dimension analysis: claim inventory with mixed evidence statuses, all 6 dimensions scored, spike/PoC recommendations for unvalidated claims, blocker analysis, verdict, and updated risk register.
expectations: Claim inventory extracts technical claims with evidence tags ([CODIGO], [CONFIG], [INFERENCIA])
Kafka experience gap classified as AT RISK or UNVALIDATED with evidence
All 6 dimensions scored 1-5: Technical, Organizational, Timeline, Financial, Regulatory, Operational
Organizational feasibility flags Conway's Law implications
Spike/PoC recommendations include MUST-DO for critical unvalidated claims
Verdict is FEASIBLE WITH CONDITIONS (not unconditional given skill gap)
Evidence tags present on all assertions

evals.name: multiple-claims-refuted
evals.prompt: Fact-check the scenario: we plan to migrate our COBOL mainframe to serverless AWS Lambda in 6 months with our current team of 3 COBOL developers.
evals.expected_output: Multiple claims refuted: timeline unrealistic, team skill mismatch severe, serverless for COBOL migration has no proven patterns. Recommends pivoting to alternative scenario.
expectations: Multiple claims classified as REFUTED or AT RISK
Timeline feasibility scores very low (COBOL-to-serverless in 6 months with 3 devs)
Organizational feasibility flags critical skill gap (COBOL developers vs serverless)
Technical feasibility questions proven patterns for COBOL-to-Lambda migration
Verdict is NOT FEASIBLE or FEASIBLE WITH CONDITIONS requiring major pivot
Recommends alternative scenario from Phase 3 options

evals.name: no-codebase-access
evals.prompt: Run feasibility analysis for modernizing a healthcare claims processing system. We have the architecture documents but no codebase access.
evals.expected_output: Paper analysis only: all technical claims marked as [INFERENCIA], increased uncertainty flagged, regulatory dimension (HIPAA) given extra weight.
expectations: All technical claims marked as [INFERENCIA] due to no codebase access
Increased uncertainty explicitly documented in verdict
Regulatory feasibility (HIPAA) thoroughly analyzed from documentation
Spike/PoC recommendations include codebase access as prerequisite
Confidence in technical dimension lower than with code access
Does not fabricate code-level evidence

evals.name: time-pressure-abbreviated
evals.prompt: Stress-test our Kubernetes migration approach. We need a quick feasibility check by end of day - just the claims and verdict.
evals.expected_output: Abbreviated S1+S5 delivery: claim inventory and feasibility verdict only. Flags risk of skipping full 6D analysis.
expectations: Delivers only S1 (claim inventory) and S5 (verdict)
Explicitly flags risk of skipping full 6-dimension analysis
Claims still have evidence tags even in abbreviated form
Verdict includes conditions and caveats about limited analysis depth
Does not fabricate dimension scores it didn't analyze

evals.name: all-claims-validated
evals.prompt: Verify technical claims for adding a Redis caching layer to our existing Node.js Express API. Team has 2 years Redis experience, existing Docker infrastructure, and 3-month timeline.
evals.expected_output: Rare case: most or all claims validated with evidence. Documents evidence, proceeds with confidence, recommends reduced contingency.
expectations: Most claims classified as VALIDATED with supporting evidence
Team experience (2 years Redis) validated against organizational dimension
Timeline (3 months for caching layer) assessed as realistic for scope
Infrastructure (existing Docker) supports operational feasibility
Verdict is FEASIBLE (unconditional or with minor conditions)
Recommends reduced contingency budget given validated claims
Does not fabricate risks where evidence supports feasibility
